notes: Confirmed: SKPath has no native equality comparison. SkPath::operator== exists in C++ but is not exposed through the C API (sk_path.h) or C# wrapper. Object.Equals(object) on SKPath performs reference equality only. The feature is absent in 2.88.9, 3.119.2, and main branch source. The SVG comparison workaround (ToSvgPathData()) works but is lossy — it doesn't preserve fill type, conic weights, or full float precision.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console project with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 and write repro code that uses reflection to check for SKPath.Equals(SKPath), IEquatable<SKPath>, and operator==
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.9
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded. PackageReference for 'SkiaSharp' version '2.88.9' added.

filesCreated.description: Repro code that checks SKPath for Equals(SKPath), IEquatable, operator==, and tests reference vs content equality

reproductionSteps.description: Run repro with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 — verify SKPath only has Object.Equals (reference equality), no IEquatable, no operator==
reproductionSteps.output:
SkiaSharp version: 2.88.0.0

--- All Equals methods on SKPath ---
  Object.Equals(Object obj) -> Boolean

SKPath.Equals(SKPath) found on: Object
SKPath implements IEquatable<SKPath>: False
SKPath has operator==: False

path1.Equals(path2) (reference equality): False
path1.Equals(path1) (same reference): True

Workaround - SVG comparison:
  path1 SVG: M0 0L100 0L100 100L0 0Z
  path2 SVG: M0 0L100 0L100 100L0 0Z
  SVG equal: True

SUCCESS: test completed

reproductionSteps.description: Check main branch source for sk_path_equals in C API and SKPath.Equals in C# wrapper — no matches found, feature not implemented on main
reproductionSteps.output: No matches found in C API (sk_path.h, sk_path.cpp) or C# wrapper (SKPath.cs). The SkPath::operator== in C++ is not plumbed through any SkiaSharp layer.

versionResults.notes: SKPath has only Object.Equals (reference equality). No IEquatable<SKPath>, no operator==, no native path comparison.
Same gap in latest stable. SKPath still has only Object.Equals with reference equality.
C API (sk_path.h) has no sk_path_equals. SKPath.cs has no Equals/IEquatable. Feature not implemented on main branch.

actionability.reason: Well-scoped feature request. Skia C++ has SkPath::operator== but it is not exposed through C API or C# wrapper. Implementation requires adding sk_path_equals to C API and SKPath.Equals/IEquatable<SKPath> to C#.

actions.description: Acknowledge feature request with investigation findings and workarounds

proposedResponse.body:
Confirmed that `SKPath` has no native equality comparison.

**Investigation findings:**
- `SKPath.Equals()` currently uses `Object.Equals` (reference equality only)
- `SKPath` does not implement `IEquatable<SKPath>`
- No `operator==` / `operator!=` overloads exist
- The C API (`sk_path.h`) has no `sk_path_equals` function
- Skia's C++ `SkPath::operator==` does exist and compares FillType, verbs, points, and conic weights

Tested on SkiaSharp 2.88.9, 3.119.2, and main branch — the gap exists in all versions.

**Workarounds:**
1. **SVG string comparison** (approximate): `path1.ToSvgPathData() == path2.ToSvgPathData()` — note this loses fill type info, converts conics to quadratics, and truncates float precision
2. **Manual iterator comparison** (correct but slow): iterate both paths with `CreateRawIterator()` and compare verbs, points, and weights

Implementing this would require plumbing `SkPath::operator==` through the C API as `sk_path_equals` and exposing it as `SKPath.Equals(SKPath)` with `IEquatable<SKPath>` support.

workarounds: SVG string comparison: path1.ToSvgPathData() == path2.ToSvgPathData() — approximate, loses fill type and conic weights
Manual iterator comparison: iterate both paths with CreateRawIterator() and compare verbs/points/weights — correct but slow due to P/Invoke per verb
